results.identified_issue: Root cause is id-sharing between the trailing next-question card and the incoming question's page 0. On auto-advance, advanceToNextPage sets currentPageID to nextQ.id (trailing card of the old feed); handleLanding then calls select(), which rebuilds the deck and reassigns currentPageID to that same value. Because the value is unchanged, neither .task(id:) nor the feed's .onChange(of: currentPageID) scroll re-fires, while the LazyVStack content swaps under a preserved scroll offset — reindexing the shared-id card from the bottom of the old feed to the top of the new one and drifting the viewport onto the first answer. The picker path (select completingCurrent:false) is immune because there currentPageID genuinely changes value. Fix adds a feedScrollNonce bumped in select() and observed by the feed to re-drive proxy.scrollTo(currentPageID) after the rebuild. Alternative rejected: briefly niling currentPageID to force a change would stop the player (task nil -> player.stop) and flicker. UNVERIFIED on device — I did not build (vigil builds/deploys the pushed commit); the fix is reasoned from the SwiftUI scroll/layout behavior, so please confirm on device that the new question is read fully before it scrolls to answer 1, and that the picker and lock/bounce behavior are unchanged.
results.summary: Kept the feed on the question card during a handoff instead of jumping to the first answer
results.detailed_description: You were right — on a question switch the feed skipped straight to the first answer instead of reading the new question. The cause: the 'next question' card at the bottom of one question's feed and the top card of the next question are the same page (same id). When the app hands off, it rebuilds the feed for the new question but keeps the scroll where it was, so that shared card slides from the bottom of the old feed to the top of the new one and the screen ends up parked on the first answer. And because the handoff re-sets the current page to the id it was already on, the code never re-scrolled to fix it. I added a small trigger that forces the feed to snap back to the question card after that rebuild, so now the question card stays put and reads out, and only then does it auto-scroll down to the first argument. The regular question picker already behaved correctly and is untouched. One caveat: I couldn't build/run it here (the build-and-deploy watcher does that from the pushed commit), so please give it a listen on the device to confirm the new question reads fully before it advances.

description: When it switches questions, it automatically goes to the first answer to a question. That shouldn't happen. It should stay on the question until it's finished reading the question. and then it can then you can auto scroll down
